name: Sierra de la Demanda Greenway and Arlanzón Reservoir Loop
name_original: Pineda de la Sierra desde la presa del embalse de Úzquiza — circular de gravel por la Vía Verde de la Sierra de la Demanda
description: Tackle the difficult 32.5-mile Sierra de la Demanda Greenway and Arlanzón Reservoir Loop, a touring cycling route with 1857 feet of elevatio
region_intro: Touring cycling around Embalse de Úzquiza is set within the Montes de Oca region of Burgos, Spain, featuring the Embalse de Úzquiza reservoir, which resembles a mountain lake. The area offers diverse landscapes, including extensive pine and oak forests, the Arlanza River valley, and traditional stone villages. Routes often follow the Vía Verde de la Sierra de la Demanda, a converted railway line, providing varied terrain from smooth tarmac to gravel sections. Best touring cycling routes around Embalse de Úzquiza The most popular touring cycling route is Sierra de la Demanda Greenway and Arlanzón Reservoir Loop, a 32.5 miles (52.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 23 minutes to complete. This route incorporates sections of the Vía Verde, passing through forests and alongside the Arlanzón Reservoir. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Arlanzón Reservoir – Pineda de la Sierra loop from Tenadas de los Quemados, a difficult 26.9 miles (43.3 km) path. This route offers views of the reservoir and leads through the surrounding natural areas, including forested sections. Local touring cyclists also love the Arlanzón Reservoir – Arlanzón Reservoir loop from Villasur de Herreros, a 26.2 miles (42.1 km) trail leading through the reservoir's perimeter and nearby villages, often completed in about 2 hours 48 minutes. Touring cycling around Embalse de Úzquiza is defined by the reservoir's waters, the Vía Verde de la Sierra de la Demanda, and ancient juniper forests. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes with significant elevation gains. The routes in Embalse de Úzquiza are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 300 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Embalse de Úzquiza's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Embalse de Úzquiza?
A: There are over 18 touring cycling routes around Embalse de Úzquiza, catering to various skill levels. You'll find options ranging from easy paths to more challenging rides with significant elevation gains.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Embalse de Úzquiza?
A: The terrain around Embalse de Úzquiza is quite diverse. Many routes follow the &lt;a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Greenway_of_the_Sierra_de_la_Demanda" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de la Sierra de la Demanda&lt;/a&gt;, offering a mix of well-maintained gravel sections and smooth tarmac. While generally featuring gentle gradients typical of former railway lines, some detours around closed tunnels can present challenging climbs. You'll cycle through pine and oak forests, alongside mountain streams, and past traditional stone villages.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in the Embalse de Úzquiza area?
A: Yes, the area offers several easier routes perfect for beginners or families. There are 2 routes specifically rated as easy, providing a more relaxed cycling experience. These paths often utilize the gentler sections of the Vía Verde, allowing you to enjoy the scenery without overly strenuous climbs.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I can see while cycling around Embalse de Úzquiza?
A: Beyond the Embalse de Úzquiza reservoir itself, which resembles a mountain lake, you can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027767035/sierra-de-la-demanda-greenway-and-arlanzon-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de la Sierra de la Demanda&lt;/a&gt;, which winds through extensive pine and oak forests. Nearby, the Sabinares del Arlanza - La Yecla Natural Park features ancient juniper forests and the dramatic La Yecla gorge. You might also spot griffon vultures in the rocky terrain of the natural park.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Embalse de Úzquiza?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes in the area are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20150212" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arlanzón Reservoir – Arlanzón Reservoir loop from Villasur de Herreros&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular option, taking you around the reservoir and through nearby villages.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Embalse de Úzquiza?
A: The region generally offers pleasant cycling conditions during spring and autumn, when temperatures are milder and the natural landscapes are vibrant. Summer can be warm, especially in the midday sun, while winter might bring colder temperatures and potentially less predictable weather, though cycling is possible year-round.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Embalse de Úzquiza?
A: The touring cycling routes around Embalse de Úzquiza are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 40 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, the tranquility of the Vía Verde, and the blend of natural beauty with historical sites. The variety of terrain, from smooth paths to more challenging sections, is also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Absolutely. For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, there are 9 routes rated as difficult. These routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31873598" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arlanzón Reservoir – Pineda de la Sierra loop from Tenadas de los Quemados&lt;/a&gt;, often feature significant elevation gains and can include unpaved or more technical sections, requiring very good fitness.

Q: Can I find amenities like cafes or accommodation along the touring cycling routes?
A: While the routes traverse natural and rural areas, you will often pass through charming traditional stone villages. These villages, particularly along the Vía Verde, may offer opportunities for cafes, local eateries, and sometimes accommodation, providing convenient stops for refreshments and rest.

Q: Are there historical sites or cultural attractions accessible by bike in the region?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. Cycling routes often lead through or near traditional villages that showcase rural architecture. The area around the Arlanza River boasts heritage treasures like the ruins of the San Pedro de Arlanza monastery and the picturesque village of Covarrubias. The Vía Verde itself was an old mining railway, and its construction led to the discovery of the UNESCO World Heritage Site of Atapuerca.

Q: Is it possible to access the touring cycling routes using public transport?
A: While specific public transport links directly to every trailhead might be limited due to the rural nature of the region, some larger towns and villages along or near the routes may have bus connections. Planning ahead and checking local transport schedules is recommended if you intend to use public transport to reach your starting point.

Q: Where can I park my car when starting a touring cycling route around Embalse de Úzquiza?
A: Parking is generally available in the villages and towns that serve as common starting points for the routes, such as Villasur de Herreros or near the Arlanzón Reservoir. Look for designated parking areas or inquire locally upon arrival to ensure proper parking for your vehicle.
tips: Arlanzón is a small and charming town that forms the entrance to the mountain and the beginning of the Vía Verde de la Sierra de la Demanda. If you pass through here, do not hesitate to visit the Romanesque church of San Miguel Arcángel.

Beautiful greenway of an old logging railway, and its short but striking history, impressive 52 km, with landscape changes, tunnels. From Burgos I did not manage to do it all in one day, I went with panniers and spent the night on the river beach of Riocavado (free camping) already in the heart of the Sierra de la Demand

It is one of the two reservoirs that supply water to the entire region of Burgos. If you are pedaling along the greenway, it is a perfect area to rest enjoying the views of the sheet of water and the spaciousness.
Nice stretch of greenway where you pedal alongside the swamp. You cross an iron footbridge and a beautiful riverside forest.
The entire road that passes north of the reservoir offers great views. Here there is a plain where you can calmly stop and enjoy the surroundings.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to cycle the Sierra de la Demanda Greenway and Arlanzón Reservoir Loop?
faqs.answer: This touring bicycle route is approximately 52.4 km (32.5 miles) long and generally takes around 3 hours and 23 minutes to complete. However, this duration can vary based on your cycling pace, fitness level, and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery or take breaks.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginner cyclists?
faqs.answer: While the Sierra de la Demanda Greenway itself has gentle slopes, the full loop incorporating the Arlanzón Reservoir is rated as difficult for touring cycling. It involves significant elevation changes (over 560 meters up) and varied terrain, including forest tracks and potentially difficult unpaved segments. It's not considered an 'initiation route,' and some sections may require very good fitness or even pushing your bike, especially with touring gear.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied. You'll primarily find compacted earth or gravel on the Greenway sections. However, the loop also includes forest tracks, some paved secondary and mountain roads, and unpaved segments. Be prepared for a mix of surfaces, some of which can be challenging.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers stunning natural beauty. You'll cycle through dense oak, beech, and Scots pine forests, open pastures, and enjoy picturesque views of the Arlanzón River. A major highlight is the expansive Beautiful views over the Arlanzón reservoir, and the Viewpoint - Úzquiza Reservoir. You'll also pass through charming villages like Pineda de la Sierra with its mountain-style architecture.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I encounter on the trail?
faqs.answer: The region is rich in biodiversity. You might spot birds of prey, squirrels, foxes, and deer. It's also known to be home to the elusive Iberian wolf, making it an excellent trail for nature enthusiasts.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The route is celebrated for its lush environment, influenced by humidity from the nearby Cantabrian mountain range. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, making them ideal for cycling. Summers can be warm, and winters may bring colder conditions or snow at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Where are the main access points and parking for the route?
faqs.answer: The route can be accessed from various points, often starting near the Arlanzón Reservoir or villages like Pineda de la Sierra. A notable starting point is near the Start of the Vía Verde de la Sierra de la Demanda. Parking facilities are typically available in or near these villages and access points, though specific details may vary.

faqs.question: Does the route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route integrates sections of several official trails. You'll find yourself on parts of the Ruta del Sureste, the Vía Verde de la Sierra de la Demanda, and the Vía Verde de la Sierra de la Demanda BTT. It also briefly touches on Etapa 1 Pineda de la Sierra - Alarcia and Etapa 12 Barbadillo de Herreros - Pineda de la Sierra, among others.

faqs.question: Is the Sierra de la Demanda Greenway and Arlanzón Reservoir Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, greenways and natural areas in Spain are dog-friendly, but dogs should be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other users. Always clean up after your pet. Specific regulations for the Espacio Natural de la Sierra de la Demanda should be checked.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or is there an entrance fee for the Espacio Natural de la Sierra de la Demanda?
faqs.answer: The route passes through the Espacio Natural de la Sierra de la Demanda. Typically, there are no entrance fees for accessing natural parks in Spain for recreational activities like cycling. However, if you plan any specific activities like camping or hunting, permits might be required. For general cycling, permits are usually not necessary, but it's always wise to respect local regulations and signage.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes through charming villages such as Pineda de la Sierra. These villages often offer local amenities, including cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options where you can refuel and rest. The town of Arlanzón is also nearby.
